text.title: trade-off-quality-attributes Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for trade-off-quality-attributes. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every architectural decision that improves a quality attribute MUST explicitly name which other attribute(s) it degrades and whether that trade-off is acceptable for the current system context. The "Consequences" section of every ADR MUST include a gain/loss pair for each affected attribute.
rule.rationale: Hidden trade-offs (not documenting sacrifices) is the most common cause of production failures that "nobody predicted". Making them explicit converts implicit organizational knowledge into reviewable artifacts.

rule.statement: Every data storage decision MUST state which CAP trade-off is acceptable for the domain. Payment systems and financial ledgers MUST use CP databases. User preference, activity feeds, and session data CAN use AP databases. Mixed requirements MUST use a hybrid: CP for transactional data, AP for read-optimized projections.
rule.rationale: Choosing AP for a payment ledger causes data loss on partition recovery. Choosing CP for a social feed adds unnecessary latency. Mismatched CAP choice is the most common source of data consistency incidents.
